symptoms.symptom_name: Acute urinary retention — no urine passed for two days with painful suprapubic fullness

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute urinary retention secondary to bladder outlet obstruction
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Complete inability to void with painful distension defines acute retention.
Malignant prostatic enlargement is a cause of outlet obstruction and retention.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive weak stream and hesitancy over the past year

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic obstructive lower urinary tract symptoms indicate longstanding prostatic outflow obstruction.
Non-specific — shared with benign prostatic hyperplasia; malignancy needs the constitutional/bony features to distinguish.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unremitting deep lumbar/pelvic bone pain, worse at night, unrelieved by paracetamol
symptoms.duration: about one month, acutely worse tonight

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Unremitting night-time axial bone pain unrelieved by simple analgesia is a classic red flag for bony metastases.
Constant night pain with constitutional features is atypical for benign mechanical back pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss (~8 kg) and profound anorexia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Unintentional weight loss with anorexia is a cardinal constitutional red flag for malignancy.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: benign prostatic hyperplasia as sole cause
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Benign obstruction does not cause cachexia or weight loss.
Non-specific; may reflect pelvic lymphatic/venous compromise or low albumin in advanced disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: No leg weakness, saddle numbness or bowel incontinence (cord-compression screen negative at this contact)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: malignant spinal cord / cauda equina compression
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Currently no neurological deficit, but a negative screen does not remove the standing threat with axial bone metastases; must be actively safety-netted.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tense, dull, exquisitely tender distended bladder palpable to mid-abdomen

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable distended bladder confirms retention — a clinician examination finding, not available via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hard, irregular, asymmetrical, nodular prostate fixed to surrounding tissue on digital rectal examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hard, irregular, fixed nodular prostate on DRE is highly suspicious for malignancy — a clinician examination finding, sealed and unavailable via telehealth.
Cachectic, chronically unwell appearance supports advanced systemic disease — clinician-observed, only partly inferable from patient-reported weight loss.
Tachycardia likely reflects pain and distress from retention; measured vitals are clinician-obtained and sealed — no home device offered.

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated (in keeping with advanced prostate malignancy)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed opportunity to support the malignancy diagnosis and stage disease.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A markedly elevated PSA supports advanced prostate malignancy — a laboratory investigation, unavailable in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Malignant acinar adenocarcinoma on transrectal ultrasound-guided biopsy

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Histological confirmation is deferred to the specialist pathway; not a telehealth or acute action.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Histology is the confirmatory gold standard — a future specialist investigation, never available via telehealth in real time.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person bladder decompression (urethral catheterisation) with drained volume measurement
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Relieves acute retention and protects renal function — performed in person / in the ED, not via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Renal function bloods (urea, electrolytes, creatinine, eGFR)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess for obstructive (post-renal) acute kidney injury from prolonged retention.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum PSA, calcium and full blood count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Support the malignancy diagnosis, detect hypercalcaemia of malignancy and anaemia; obtained in the emergency/inpatient setting.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Spinal / bone imaging (MRI whole spine if any neurological signs; bone scan / CT for staging)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Evaluate skeletal metastases and exclude malignant spinal cord compression, especially if neurological signs develop.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: AI Doctor attempting to talk the patient through self-catheterisation or ordering a biopsy remotely
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Catheterisation is an in-person procedure and biopsy is a specialist pathway — neither is a telehealth action; the correct move is emergency escalation.
